target.title: [codex] fix Claude AskUserQuestion bridge
target.body:
## Summary

- Handle Claude `AskUserQuestion` control requests in the Claude live stdio runner instead of routing them through the native exec approval path.
- Render Claude question/options payloads as plain reply-surface prompts, then map the next active-run queued message back into Claude's documented `updatedInput.answers` shape.
- Keep `updatedInput.answers` values as strings, including comma-separated strings for multi-select answers per Claude's SDK contract.
- Route pending Claude prompt answers ahead of normal `messages.queue.mode` follow-up/collect queueing, while leaving ordinary non-prompt queue behavior intact.
- Add a deterministic no-surface fallback that denies `AskUserQuestion` and tells Claude to ask directly in plain text.

## Root Cause

Claude sends `AskUserQuestion` through the same `can_use_tool` control request channel as native tool approval. OpenClaw's Claude live-session handler only treated those requests as permission decisions, and CLI reply backends did not expose a queued-message path for user answers. On surfaces without an interactive picker, Claude's structured question could not be answered deterministically.

## Review Fixes

- Fixed multi-select answer serialization: selected labels now return as one comma-separated string instead of a JSON array.
- Added `ReplyBackendHandle.isAwaitingUserInput` and `isReplyRunAwaitingUserInputForSessionId` so admission can distinguish a pending Claude prompt answer from ordinary user follow-up text.
- Updated prepared-reply admission so an active reply run waiting for user input steers the next matching reply even when queue mode is `followup` or `collect`.
- Added coverage for pending prompt-answer admission in `steer`, `followup`, and `collect` queue modes.

## Impact

Agents like Sam can now ask interview/clarifying questions through normal OpenClaw reply surfaces. If a surface cannot collect structured input, Claude gets a clear plain-text fallback instruction instead of depending on an unsupported picker.

## Verification

Clean PR worktree: `/Users/tim/JSI/GIT/openclaw-claude-ask-user-question`

- `corepack pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`
- `git diff --check`
- `rg -n "\\t" src/agents/cli-runner.spawn.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner/claude-live-session.ts src/auto-reply/reply/reply-run-registry.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-run.ts src/auto-reply/reply/reply-run-registry.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-run.media-only.test.ts || true` -> no matches
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/cli-runner.spawn.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/reply-run-registry.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-run.media-only.test.ts` -> 2 Vitest shards passed; 150 tests passed
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo` -> passed

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Claude `AskUserQuestion` prompts in live stdio CLI runs now have deterministic OpenClaw handling: reply-capable runs can bridge the question through the reply surface, and no-surface runs tell Claude to fall back to plain text instead of depending on an unsupported picker.

Real environment tested after the review fixes: commit `e4071a76e92df1582befc421f2ef29fefda9b10a`, OpenClaw `2026.6.2 (e4071a7)`, real Claude CLI `claude 2.1.168`, model `claude-cli/claude-sonnet-4-6`, isolated `OPENCLAW_HOME=/tmp/openclaw-proof-home.emxmMM`.

Live no-surface command:

```sh
OPENCLAW_HOME=/tmp/openclaw-proof-home.emxmMM corepack pnpm openclaw agent --local --agent main --model claude-cli/claude-sonnet-4-6 --session-key agent:main:ask-user-question-proof --message "This is a runtime smoke test for OpenClaw. Call the AskUserQuestion tool now with one question exactly 'Which mode should OpenClaw use?' and two options exactly 'Plan' and 'Build'. If the host denies that tool or says to ask in plain text, do not ask a follow-up; instead reply exactly 'ASK_USER_QUESTION_FALLBACK_OBSERVED'." --timeout 180 --json
```

Relevant redacted output:

```text
OpenClaw 2026.6.2 (e4071a7)
[agent/cli-backend] cli exec: provider=claude-cli model=claude-sonnet-4-6 promptChars=351 trigger=user useResume=false session=none resumeSession=none reuse=none historyPrompt=none
[agent/cli-backend] claude live session start: provider=claude-cli model=claude-sonnet-4-6 activeSessions=1
[agent/cli-backend] claude live session turn: provider=claude-cli model=claude-sonnet-4-6 durationMs=8047 rawLines=38 outBytes=35 outHash=80b95109022b
"finalAssistantVisibleText": "ASK_USER_QUESTION_FALLBACK_OBSERVED"
"executionTrace": { "winnerProvider": "claude-cli", "winnerModel": "claude-sonnet-4-6", "runner": "cli" }
```

Reply-capable prompt bridge proof from focused runtime coverage:

- `src/agents/cli-runner.spawn.test.ts` simulates Claude's live `control_request` for `AskUserQuestion`, renders the prompt through `onUserInputPrompt`, answers via `queueReplyRunMessage("live-session-ask", "1: 2\n2: 1, 2")`, and asserts Claude receives:
  - `updatedInput.answers["Which thread is this?"] === "All"`
  - `updatedInput.answers["Which modes?"] === "Plan, Build"`
- `src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-run.media-only.test.ts` covers pending prompt-answer admission for `messages.queue.mode` values `steer`, `followup`, and `collect`, asserting each routes through active steering when the active reply backend is awaiting user input.
- `src/auto-reply/reply/reply-run-registry.test.ts` covers the pending-user-input registry signal only reporting true for an active running backend.

What was not tested: a live external Telegram/Slack/etc. round trip with a human answering the bridged prompt. The reply-capable path is covered by focused runtime tests that exercise the same Claude live-session control response and reply-run queue plumbing.
target.author: jsi-tross
